skill.name: sql-queries-tool
skill.description: Expert SQL query generation for DBX Studio. Use when writing, optimizing, or debugging SQL queries against user database connections.
skill.summary: AI-powered SQL query generation with dialect support and safety rules
skill.author: Dbxstudio

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill file containing SQL query patterns and best practices. The static analyzer flagged 24 potential issues, but manual review confirms all are false positives. The backtick characters detected are markdown code fences for SQL examples. No cryptographic algorithms or system reconnaissance is present. The skill explicitly restricts queries to SELECT/WITH only, making it safe for database operations.

content.value_statement: Write optimized SQL queries for any database. AI understands your schema and generates dialect-appropriate queries with safety built in.
actual_capabilities: Generate SELECT queries with proper LIMIT and pagination
Write complex CTEs and window functions
Support multiple SQL dialects (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Snowflake, BigQuery)
Validate schema existence before query execution
Provide query explanations and performance tips
Auto-quote all SQL identifiers for safety
limitations: Only generates read-only SELECT and WITH queries
Cannot execute DDL or DML statements
Requires valid database connection in DBX Studio
Cannot create or modify database schema

use_cases.title: Data Analysis
use_cases.description: Generate aggregation queries, time-series analysis, and reporting queries
use_cases.target_user: Data analysts needing quick insights from databases

use_cases.title: Application Development
use_cases.description: Write efficient database queries for application backends

use_cases.title: Database Exploration
use_cases.description: Preview table data and understand schema relationships

prompt_templates.title: Simple Data Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the first 10 rows from the users table

prompt_templates.title: Aggregation Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate total revenue per month for the last 6 months

prompt_templates.title: Complex CTE Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the top 5 customers by order count with their total spending using a CTE

prompt_templates.title: Window Function Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Show running total of transactions per user ordered by date

output_examples.input: Show me orders from last week
output_examples.output: There are **1,247 orders** from last week totaling **$45,678.90** in revenue. The average order value is **$36.62**.

output_examples.input: What columns does the products table have?
output_examples.output: The products table has 8 columns: id (integer), name (varchar), price (decimal), category_id (integer), stock_quantity (integer), created_at (timestamp), updated_at (timestamp), is_active (boolean).

best_practices: Always use read_schema or describe_table first to understand the schema
Add LIMIT unless user explicitly requests all rows
Prefer CTEs over subqueries for readability
anti_patterns: Never generate INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DROP statements
Avoid SELECT * in production - specify needed columns
Do not skip LIMIT on queries returning potentially large datasets
Do not assume column names without checking schema first

faq.question: Can this skill modify my database?
faq.answer: No. The skill only generates SELECT and WITH (CTE) queries. No INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or DDL statements are permitted.

faq.question: Which databases are supported?
faq.answer: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, Snowflake, BigQuery, and Databricks. The skill auto-detects the dialect from your connection.

faq.question: How does the AI know my schema?
faq.answer: The skill uses read_schema and describe_table tools to inspect your database structure before generating queries.

faq.question: Can I get raw SQL output?
faq.answer: Yes, ask explicitly with "show me the query" or "how did you write this" and the AI will display the SQL.

faq.question: What if my query is slow?
faq.answer: The skill can analyze query performance and suggest optimizations like adding indexes or rewriting for better execution plans.

faq.question: Is this safe for production databases?
faq.answer: Yes. All queries are read-only and the skill validates schema existence before execution to prevent errors.
